summary: Mechanical cleanup: remove three call-site fingerprints of an @deprecated dependency. No investigation phase needed; predecessor WO already exhaustively documented the dependency surface and confirmed it is genuinely unused post-substrate-pivot. Plan structure: 2 atomic commits (production + test) plus a verification step.

indexing_orchestrator_ts.current_shape: class IndexingOrchestrator carries a private analyzerService field that is assigned in the constructor but never read after WO-RAG-INDEX-SINGLE-ANALYZER-SLICE-001 replaced the second analyzerService.analyze() call with a graph.json read. Field is JSDoc-flagged @deprecated.

rag_index_cli.current_shape: CLI entrypoint constructs an AnalyzerService instance solely to feed it to the IndexingOrchestrator constructor. The instance is otherwise unused in this file post-pivot.

indexing_pipeline_test.current_shape: Test fixture constructs IndexingOrchestrator with a mockAnalyzerService whose analyze method is a vi.fn() that is never invoked or asserted on. Lines 140-146 contain a comment block claiming the mock acts as a 'regression sentinel' — but the assertion `expect(analyzerService.analyze).not.toHaveBeenCalled()` referenced in that comment does not actually exist in test source. The mock is therefore dead weight, not a load-bearing invariant.

callsites.text: regression-sentinel comment block — describes an assertion that does not exist in source

other_consumers_check.method: Grep src/ for `new IndexingOrchestrator` and `analyzerService` (predecessor-WO grep already exhaustive)
other_consumers_check.result: Only three sites identified above. BreakingChangeDetector uses AnalyzerService independently and is outside this WO's diff radius (different file, different class).
other_consumers_check.confidence: high — predecessor WO ARCHIVED.md confirms diff radius was src/integration/rag/* only

investigation_targets.rationale: Predecessor WO-RAG-INDEX-SINGLE-ANALYZER-SLICE-001 ARCHIVED.md already enumerated the dependency surface post-pivot. Three callsites are known with line numbers; no probe scripts or behavioral investigation needed.

implementation_strategy.approach: Two atomic commits plus tsc/test verification.

commits.label: C1: production cleanup
operations: Delete L360 private analyzerService field declaration
Update class JSDoc header comment if it references AnalyzerService dependency
Delete L437 const analyzerService = new AnalyzerService(...)
Delete L441 analyzerService argument from new IndexingOrchestrator(...)
commits.tsc_check: tsc --noEmit clean both configs after this commit

commits.label: C2: test cleanup
operations: Delete L147-149 mockAnalyzerService declaration + vi.fn() analyze
Delete or condense L140-146 regression-sentinel commentary (the sentinel never existed; misleading comment)
Verify all assertions in this test file still PASS
commits.tsc_check: tsc --noEmit clean both configs after this commit

verification: Verify AC-05a + AC-05b dual-AC identity tests still PASS (commit affff3a fixture)

risks.description: Hidden callsite somewhere in the repo that constructs IndexingOrchestrator with analyzerService and is missed by the grep
risks.impact: tsc compile failure on C1, surfaced immediately
risks.mitigation: tsc gate after C1 will catch any missed callsite. Predecessor WO already grep-confirmed exhaustive.

risks.description: Removing the mock breaks an assertion that DOES live somewhere in the test file (i.e., the commentary at L140-146 is real, not stale)
risks.mitigation: Inspect test file fully before C2; if the assertion exists, decide whether to keep the mock as a true regression sentinel (drop the JSDoc @deprecated, keep the field but unused — alternative path) or delete the assertion if it is itself dead weight

risks.description: AC-05a/b vitest fixture (affff3a) constructs IndexingOrchestrator differently and breaks
risks.mitigation: Fixture is in __tests__ tree; same boundary; if it constructs IndexingOrchestrator with analyzerService it gets the same edit treatment in C2

expected_outcome.loc_delta: net negative ~-15 LOC (5-8 deletions in production code, 5-7 deletions + ~5 commentary lines deleted in test)
expected_outcome.test_pass_rate: 100% — all existing tests continue to pass; dual-AC identity preserved
